name: Chocolate Almond Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Unsalted Butter, melted, 30 g, 1 oz
recipeInstructions:
Filling:
2 pounds Cream Cheese -- 1 kg
1 1/2 cups Sugar -- 375 g, 12 oz
4 Eggs
5 ounces Bittersweet Chocolate -- melted, 155 g
1/2 teaspoon Vanilla Extract
The last word in decadence, Hazelnuts, pecans, or your favorite nut can be
used instead of almonds in the crust. I've also known to chop up 2 ounces
of bittersweet chocolate - that's about 60 g - to add to the filling.
Now maybe that's the last word in decadence!!
In a food processor, blend the almonds and sugar to a coarse meal. Add
the butter and almond extract and process until fully blended. Press the
mixture into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan, bringing it up the
sides to a height of 1/4 inch. Chill for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) or gas mark 4.
To prepare the filling, cut the cream cheese into 8 or 10 pieces and place
in a mixer. Scrape the sides and bottom of the bowl frequently, blending
until smooth. Add the sugar and mix thoroughly. Add the eggs one at a
time, mixing well after each addition. When all the eggs are fully
incorporated, drizzle in the chocolate, mixing to blend.
Turn off the machine and scrape the bottom and sides down well.
With the machine back on, add the vanilla and blend.
Pour the mixture into the crust. Bake for 50 to 60 minutes, or until a
tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Let the cheesecake cool
and refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
Serve with whipped cream.
Serves 8 - 12
